title: Java Pass-by-Value: Primitive vs. Reference
description: A flowchart illustrating the difference between passing primitive types and object references to a method, demonstrating that Java is strictly pass-by-value.
relationships: Caller passes a copy of a primitive value
Caller passes a copy of an object reference
Method modifies the copy of the primitive (no effect on original)
Method uses the copy of the reference to modify the Heap Object (affects original object)
Method reassigns the copy of the reference (no effect on original reference)
